source: Insurance Regulatory Authority (IRA) — Motor Insurance Minimum Premium Rates

vehicleClasses.name: Private Motor Vehicle
vehicleClasses.description: Privately owned vehicles for personal use

thirdParty.description: Third Party Only — minimum legal requirement
thirdParty.coverageNotes: Covers injury/death to third parties and damage to third party property up to KES 20 million

comprehensive.description: Comprehensive cover — percentage of vehicle value
comprehensive.coverageNotes: Covers own damage, theft, fire, third party liability, and windscreen

thirdParty.description: Third Party Only for commercial vehicles

comprehensive.description: Comprehensive cover — higher rates due to commercial risk

vehicleClasses.name: Public Service Vehicle (PSV)
vehicleClasses.description: Matatus, buses, taxis — licensed for passenger transport

vehicleClasses.name: Motorcycle (Boda Boda)
vehicleClasses.description: Two-wheeled motor vehicles including boda bodas

comprehensive.description: Comprehensive cover — high theft/accident rate for boda bodas

additionalCovers.name: Windscreen Cover
additionalCovers.description: Separate cover available for third party policies

additionalCovers.name: Political Violence & Terrorism (PVT)
additionalCovers.description: Optional add-on, recommended for commercial vehicles

additionalCovers.name: Excess Protector
additionalCovers.description: Covers the excess/deductible amount in case of a claim

notes: Third Party Only insurance is the minimum legal requirement under the Insurance (Motor Vehicle Third Party Risks) Act (Cap 405)
Driving without valid insurance is a criminal offence with a fine of up to KES 100,000 or imprisonment
IRA sets minimum premium rates — actual premiums from insurers may be higher based on risk assessment
Comprehensive premiums depend on vehicle value, age, driver history, and insurer's underwriting criteria
PSV vehicles must additionally have a valid NTSA inspection certificate and PSV badge
